ambiguous_terms: reaches 0 (in REQ-005): unclear if this refers to an exact value of 0 or any value <= 0 resulting from damage)
simultaneous damage (in REQ-002): unclear if the Anomaly burst (REQ-005) is part of the simultaneous damage phase or occurs immediately after the hero's HP is reduced
missing_rules: Anomaly trigger condition when mana is already 0: does an attack that deals damage to a hero who already has 0 mana trigger the Anomaly again?
risky_assumptions: Assume {dir: [dx, dy]} in the Move action is a relative displacement vector rather than an absolute target coordinate

questions_for_lead.q: Does the Anomaly burst trigger only on the transition from mana > 0 to mana == 0, or every time damage is taken while mana is 0?
Is the 'dir' input for the Move action intended to be a relative offset (e.g., [0, 1]) or an absolute coordinate (e.g., [0, 1])?
Does the Anomaly burst damage apply to the enemy that initiated the attack?

missing_rules: behavior when mana is already 0 at the start of an attack
handling of target IDs that exist but have hp <= 0
handling of invalid move directions (e.g., non-integers or length != 2)
behavior for move [0, 0]
action queue behavior if the queue is initially empty
underspecified_outputs: specific log text for Move failure cases
specific log text for Attack failure cases
risky_assumptions: mana always starts at a value greater than 0

questions_for_lead.q: Does 'mana reaches 0' trigger the Anomaly if the mana was already 0 before the attack began?
Are enemies with hp <= 0 immediately removed from the grid, or do they remain as obstacles for the Move action?

risky_assumptions: The 'Anomaly' burst 'upon rupture' (GDD) and 'reaches 0' (REQ-005) is assumed to be a one-time state transition, but the state object lacks a flag (e.g., 'is_ruptured') to prevent the effect from triggering on every attack while mana remains at 0.

questions_for_lead.q: Is the Anomaly burst a one-time event per game, or does it trigger every time an attack occurs while mana is 0? If it is a one-time event, we need to add a tracking property to the hero state to mark the shield as ruptured.

ambiguous_terms: reaches 0 (in REQ-005: unclear if this is a state transition from >0 to 0, or if it triggers whenever mana is 0)
missing_rules: Log entry for the Anomaly burst trigger
Log entries for failed Attack actions (target not adjacent or ID missing)
underspecified_outputs: Specific error string for REQ-001 move failures
Log output for mana shield absorption vs. HP damage transition
risky_assumptions: Assuming that enemy HP reduction is a sufficient observable for Anomaly verification without a distinct log event

questions_for_lead.q: Does the Anomaly effect trigger only on the specific turn mana drops to 0, or every single attack while mana remains 0?

conflicting_rules: REQ-001 duplicates GDD Move failure logic (grid bounds/occupied)
REQ-002 duplicates GDD Attack adjacency and simultaneous damage logic
REQ-006 duplicates GDD Defeat logic (hero.hp <= 0 priority)
REQ-007 overlaps GDD Victory logic (enemies list empty vs enemies dead and hero.hp > 0)

questions_for_lead.q: The Requirements section (REQ-001, REQ-002, REQ-006, REQ-007) largely duplicates the GDD logic; which section is the definitive source of truth for implementation?

missing_rules: Whether the Anomaly effect triggers if hero.mana is already 0 at the start of an attack
Whether Anomaly damage is logged in the action log
conflicting_rules: REQ-002 (simultaneous damage) vs REQ-005 (immediately deal anomaly_dmg): it is unclear if the Anomaly burst occurs as part of the simultaneous exchange or as a subsequent step after the exchange is resolved
risky_assumptions: Assumption that 'mana reaches 0' implies a state transition from >0 to 0, rather than simply being 0

questions_for_lead.q: Does the Anomaly burst trigger if the hero's mana is already 0 when the attack begins, or only when a hit causes it to drop to 0?
In the 'simultaneous' damage phase, does the Anomaly burst happen before, during, or after the mutual HP/mana subtraction?

ambiguous_terms: simultaneous damage (order of state update for HP/Mana vs Enemy HP)
missing_rules: Action queue behavior upon failure (does the simulation halt or skip to the next action?)
Enemy attack triggers (is the 'mutual damage' in Attack the only way the hero takes damage?)
Enemy movement rules (are enemies stationary for the duration of the simulation?)
underspecified_outputs: Log entries for Mana Shield rupture
Log entries for Anomaly burst damage
risky_assumptions: Assumption that enemies are passive and do not have their own action turns
Assumption that the 'simultaneous' damage doesn't check if the enemy died before dealing damage to the hero

questions_for_lead.q: If a Move action fails (REQ-001), should the simulator skip to the next action in the queue or terminate the entire scenario immediately?
Confirming: The only way the Hero takes damage is through the 'mutual damage' trigger during a Hero Attack action. Are there no independent enemy turns?

The 'Anomaly' burst 'upon rupture' (GDD) and 'reaches 0' (REQ-005) is assumed to be a one-time state transition, but the state object lacks a flag (e.g., 'is_ruptured') to prevent the effect from triggering on every attack while mana remains at 0.
Assuming that enemy HP reduction is a sufficient observable for Anomaly verification without a distinct log event
Assumption that 'mana reaches 0' implies a state transition from >0 to 0, rather than simply being 0
Assumption that enemies are passive and do not have their own action turns
Assumption that the 'simultaneous' damage doesn't check if the enemy died before dealing damage to the hero

Does 'mana reaches 0' trigger the Anomaly if the mana was already 0 before the attack began?
Are enemies with hp <= 0 immediately removed from the grid, or do they remain as obstacles for the Move action?
